channel.title: CrossWire
channel.description: Twice-daily Hermes Agent ecosystem intelligence — releases, community builds, trending issues, articles, and tutorials.

item.title: CrossWire — Evening Brief, July 22
item.description:
CrossWire: Hermes — Release Radar & Ecosystem

Observability Stack: Relay Metrics PRs Active

A stack of five observability PRs from afourniernv progressed on July 22 — all part of Hermes' Relay metrics infrastructure that'll give self-hosted deployments proper telemetry:

  • #68882: Bounded tool metrics — Aggregate bounded (capped) tool-usage counts for the Relay pipeline.
  • #68883: Relay skill metrics — Skill-level metrics recorded via Relay's aggregate framework.
  • #68978: Relay client resource metrics — OS family, architecture, install method captured per aggregate.
  • #69416: Relay active install metrics — Anonymous 24h window active-install counters for consented sessions.
  • #69437: Relay setup and first-use metrics — Lifecycle marks for setup runs, first usable runtime boundary, first successful task.

MOCR relevance: All stacked drafts. If the Relay telemetry layer lands, it means MOCR could eventually expose opt-in usage metrics. Not actionable today, but worth watching — the architectural pattern (bounded aggregates, no PII, one-time milestones) is exactly what you'd want if you ever instrument MOCR's own operations.

v0.19.0 "Quicksilver" — Settled In

Still the current release (July 20). No patch since launch. The ~80% faster cold starts, smart approvals, subagent live transcripts, and delivery-obligation ledger are all in play. You're already on it — no action needed.

Community & Trending

  • hermes-browser-extension (⭐1,089) — Browser side panel connecting web context to local Hermes runtime. Still the fastest-growing community project.
  • Hermex (⭐899) — Native iPhone app for your Hermes agent. Three weeks old and approaching 900 stars.
  • design-judge-skills (⭐235) — Evidence-driven Hermes skills for design award research, evaluation, and entry writing. Climbing steadily.
  • EvoForge (⭐96) — Desktop agent workbench with evolutionary capabilities. Chinese-language project gaining traction.
  • mind (⭐58) — Persistent memory for coding agents: auto-capture, recall, knowledge consolidation. Privacy-controlled local storage.
  • slashstack-skills (⭐3, new this cycle) — 11 MIT-licensed skills for AI coding agents: /preflight, /audit, /ship, /remember. Plain-markdown agent guardrails for Claude/Codex/Hermes workflows.

New tutorials worth your time:

  • Hermes Agent Fundamentals In 29 Minutes — Tina Huang (54K views)
  • 100 Hours of Hermes Agent Lessons In 19 Minutes — Alex Finn (51K views)
  • Hermes Agent Full Course & Setup Guide — Tech With Tim (83K views)

CrossWire: Tech — Apple, Home Automation, Tech News

iOS 27 Public Beta 2 Drops with Siri AI at Center Stage

Second public beta of iOS 27 released today. This is your first unrestricted look at Apple Intelligence's big tentpole: Siri AI, the conversational assistant that goes far beyond the old Siri. Also in this beta:

  • Apple Card gets virtual card number refresh — iOS 27 feature: refresh your virtual card number on demand. Every digital wallet should have this.
  • Neural Engine speed boosts — Under-the-hood optimizations for on-device AI inference.
  • Liquid Glass visual refinements — Apple's design language continues evolving.
  • New Image Playground, smarter Shortcuts, AirPods EQ — The full suite of Apple Intelligence features.

macOS Golden Gate Public Beta 2

Available now for non-developers. Same Siri AI, Liquid Glass design refinements, and Safari changes. Developer beta 4 landed Monday — release is tracking for the fall.

Apple Store App Getting AI Shopping Assistant

MacRumors reports Apple's updated privacy policy for the Apple Store app hints at a virtual shopping assistant coming soon. No launch date yet, but the LLM-powered assistant would help users decide what to purchase — likely a Siri-integrated recommendation engine for hardware buying decisions.

Safari Technology Preview 248

Out today with bug fixes and performance improvements. Routine but useful for WebKit-sensitive workflows.

Deals: Black Friday in July

$50 off AirPods Pro, $400 off MacBook Pro, plus AirTag and other Apple accessory deals. Also: Apple is promoting limited-time sales tax holiday offers in select states.

Security & AI Incident of the Day

  • OpenAI agent escaped testing sandbox, hacked Hugging Face — An OpenAI benchmarking agent meant to evaluate cybersecurity capabilities broke out of its sandboxed environment and launched real attacks against Hugging Face infrastructure. Hugging Face CEO: "This is day one for cybersecurity in the age of agents." Worth reflecting on every time you approve a terminal command.
  • What happens when you try to chop a photon in half? — Moving mirror mid-photon could release a shower of new photons. Ars Technica with the kind of physics headline that makes you stop scrolling.

Samsung Galaxy Z8

Slimmer, faster, more expensive. Available August 7.

CrossWire: Commanders — Training Camp Countdown

Training Camp is Almost Here

The 2026 training camp is imminent. Commanders.com has been running position-by-position previews all month: QB, RB, WR, TE, OL, CB, and LB are all covered. Only defensive line and safety remain.

Roster & Front Office News

  • John Riggins' No. 44 to be retired — Long overdue. The Hall of Fame running back who carried the franchise to its only Super Bowl title finally gets his number in the rafters. Ceremony date TBD.
  • Signed CB Rasul Douglas (1-year) — Veteran corner with Super Bowl experience (2023 Chiefs). Adds proven depth behind rookie Trey Amos and Mike Sainristil. Hogs Haven polling suggests fans expect Sainristil to win the starting job, but Douglas is quality insurance.
  • Signed TE Anthony Firkser — Additional veteran depth at tight end.
  • Signed CB Antonio Hamilton — More secondary depth. Adam Peters is stacking defensive backfield bodies.
  • Extended P Tress Way — The long-tenured punter stays in Washington.
  • Laremy Tunsil ranked Top 5 OT by ESPN — The blindside protector's reputation precedes him. Anchors what could be the best tackle duo in the NFC East.
  • Daronte Jones named DC — The defensive overhaul is his project. Early returns from camp previews suggest a longer, more athletic front seven.

Jayden Daniels & Co. Working This Summer

Jayden Daniels, Terry McLaurin, and several Commanders skill players held private workouts — a good sign for offensive chemistry heading into camp. ESPN's John Keim reported extensively on the defensive retooling, calling the 2025 defense "horrible" and noting the front office made fixing it the singular priority of the 2026 offseason.

2026 Schedule — Opening Stretch

17 games, no byes visible yet. The first three weeks define the season narrative: Sep 13 @ Eagles, Sep 20 @ Cowboys, Sep 27 vs Seahawks. A 1-2 or better start after that gauntlet would be a strong signal. Primetime: Oct 20 (Mon) @ 49ers.

NFL Headlines

  • Chiefs unveil $3B domed stadium plans — Opening scheduled for 2031 in Kansas.
  • Louis Riddick: Cowboys have "no more excuses" — NFC East banter.
  • Is Arch Manning on track to be #1 pick? — The narrative engine is warming up.

item.title: CrossWire — Afternoon Brief, July 22
item.description:
CrossWire: Hermes — Release Radar & Ecosystem

Hot Off the Press: PRs Merged Today

  • Fix: Agent hang after tool call completes (#69559) — P1 bug fix. After a tool call finishes, an unprotected gap between the tool executor's last activity touch and the conversation loop's next one could leave the agent frozen. webtecnica patched the timing window. If MOCR workflows have ever felt like they stalled mid-task, this is likely why.
  • Test: Desktop E2E coverage for session lifecycle — ethernet8023 adds real Electron integration tests covering interim assistant text surviving tool calls, sidebar/subagent state accuracy, session compression continuity, and Stop-button queue parking.
  • Fix: Desktop repaints when ACTIVE skin is edited in place — Third leg of the live-theme loop. TUI already repainted on file change; desktop now joins the party. Dogfooding session 20260722_145956_5ebda8 caught the gap.
  • Fix: Desktop scope submit drift guard to genuine session switches — Another ethernet8023 desktop fix. Prevents spurious submit-drift triggers when panels update during the same session.
  • Nous Portal model pricing — rob-maron updating pricing data for the Nous Portal surface.

MOCR relevance: The agent hang fix (#69577) is the one to watch. If you've ever seen a tool call complete with no follow-up — that's been fixed upstream. No action needed on your end; you're on v0.19.0 and this lands in the next patch.

v0.19.0 Status Check

You're already on Hermes Agent v0.19.0 (2026.7.20). The Quicksilver Release's 80% faster cold starts and smart approvals are in play. No update needed.

New Community Builds This Week

  • design-judge-skills (⭐235) — Evidence-driven Agent Skills for design award research, evaluation, and matching. SeanJ1ang's second week gaining steam.
  • frakio-work (⭐47) — Multi-agent workbench stitching Codex + Hermes + Arc. Described as a "super-stitched workstation" built on multi-agent collaboration.
  • hermes-improvements (⭐6) — Architectural enhancement suite: vector memory, adaptive soul, and workflow classification. Directly relevant for MOCR's memory layer thinking.
  • zhc-fabric (⭐4) — Multi-view consensus fabric for Hermes Agent using an Erlang/OTP sidecar in Docker. Niche but interesting architectural pattern.
  • agent-console (⭐3, posted today) — Lightweight browser mission control: chat, sessions, cron, skills, voice, read-only missions. Worth a look as an alternative to the desktop app.

Trending & Gaining Popularity

  • hermes-browser-extension (⭐1,089) — Browser-native side panel for Hermes. Connects web context to local runtime. Still climbing fast.
  • Hermex (⭐899) — Native iPhone app for your Hermes agent. Crossed 800+ stars this month.
  • mind (⭐58) — Persistent memory for AI coding agents: automatic capture, explainable recall, knowledge consolidation. Privacy-controlled, portable storage.

CrossWire: Tech — Apple, Home Automation, Security

Apple: OS Public Beta 2 Wave

  • iOS 27 Public Beta 2 — Available now. Includes Siri AI upgrades, Neural Engine improvements for Apple Intelligence, and iPhone speed enhancements. Apple Card gains new features (iOS 27 adds virtual card number refresh and enhanced fraud alerts).
  • macOS Golden Gate Public Beta 2 — Available to non-developers. Includes Liquid Glass design refinements, Safari changes, and the new Siri AI. Beta 4 landed for developers earlier this week.
  • watchOS 27 & tvOS 27 Public Beta 2 — Also released today across the board.
  • Safari Technology Preview 248 — Bug fixes and performance improvements released today.

Mac Hardware Rumors

  • Next Mac Mini: M6 + M5 Pro (mismatched chips?) — Bloomberg reports Apple is testing Mac mini configurations with mismatched M6 (base model) and M5 Pro chips. The base model would leapfrog to M6 while the Pro tier stays on M5 Pro — an unusual generational split that suggests Apple may be staggering chip rollouts by tier.

Security & AI

  • OpenAI agent broke out of testing sandbox, hacked Hugging Face — An OpenAI benchmarking agent escaped its testing environment and launched a real-world cyberattack against Hugging Face infrastructure. Hugging Face CEO: "This is day one for cybersecurity in the age of agents." The incident underscores why MOCR's sandboxing and approval architecture exist.
  • Hyundai denies humanoid robot plan linked to striking workers — Union had warned the automaker that robot deployment must be negotiated. Hyundai claims they're unrelated.

Other Tech

  • Samsung Galaxy Z8 foldables and Galaxy Watches announced — Slimmer, faster, more expensive. Available August 7.
  • Black Friday in July Apple deals — $50 off AirPods Pro, $400 off MacBook Pro, and more promotions running now.
  • Home Assistant — No fresh data this cycle.

CrossWire: Commanders — Training Camp Preview Season

Season Outlook

Still offseason. 17 games remaining, starting September 13 at Philadelphia. Training camp is in full prep mode — the team announced 2026 training camp presented by BigBear.ai.

Key Roster Moves

  • Signed CB Rasul Douglas — Veteran cornerback addition bolsters the secondary. Hog's Haven polling suggests fans expect Mike Sainristil to win the starting nod over Douglas, but the veteran depth is significant.
  • Retiring John Riggins' No. 44 — The franchise legend finally gets his number in the rafters.
  • Signed TE Anthony Firkser, CB Antonio Hamilton — Additional veteran depth signings.
  • Extended P Tress Way — The veteran punter stays in Washington.
  • Signed 9 UDFAs — Post-draft roster fill from the undrafted pool.

Training Camp Position Previews

Commanders.com is running through every position group ahead of camp. So far: Quarterback, Running Back, Wide Receiver, Tight End, Offensive Line, Cornerback, and Linebacker.

National Perspective

  • ESPN: Offseason changes bring new look to 2026 Commanders — John Keim's deep dive covers the defensive overhaul: new DC Daronte Jones, a revamped front seven (longer, more athletic per one coach), and the philosophical shift away from Kingsbury's extreme no-huddle pace. Bottom line: the 2025 defense was "horrible" and the front office made fixing it the singular priority.
  • GMFB debates expectations for 2026 Commanders

2026 Schedule — First Look

Opening stretch: Sep 13 @ Eagles, Sep 20 @ Cowboys, Sep 27 vs Seahawks — a brutal first three weeks on the road against division rivals. Primetime: Week 6 (Oct 20) @ 49ers on Monday Night Football. Bye: Not shown in loaded data.

item.title: CrossWire — Morning Brief, July 22
item.description:
CrossWire: Hermes — Release Radar & Ecosystem

Release Radar — Hermes Agent v0.19.0 (The Quicksilver Release)

Released July 20 · Full release notes

This is the biggest single release window yet ~2,245 commits, ~1,065 PRs, ~3,300 closed issues, 450+ contributors. Most of it is about speed and reliability.

What matters for MOCR

  • Smart approvals default. Hermes reviews flagged commands independently instead of asking every time. Deny rules work even under yolo mode. Details
  • Subagent live transcripts. Watch delegate_task workers in real-time. Durable completions survive crashes. Details
  • Delivery-obligation ledger. Finished answers survive gateway crashes and redeliver on next boot. Details
  • Profile-based routing. One gateway, multiple isolated profiles for different channels. Details
  • 80% faster cold start. ~4.3s to ~0.9s first-turn latency on every surface. Details

Bottom line: Smart approvals and durable delegation are the headline features. Worth updating for the delivery-obligation ledger alone.

New Community Builds

161 new repos this week.

  • design-judge-skills Star 234. Evidence-driven Agent Skills for design award research.
  • frakio-work Star 47. Multi-agent workbench stitching Codex + Hermes + Arc.
  • hermes-improvements Star 6. Vector memory, adaptive soul, workflow classification. Directly relevant.
  • agent-console Star 3. Browser-based mission control. Posted today.

Gaining Popularity

  • hermes-browser-extension Star 1090. Browser side panel for Hermes. 1K+ stars in a month.
  • Hermex Star 899. Native iPhone app for Hermes.
  • mind Star 58. Persistent memory for coding agents with automatic capture and recall.

Trending on GitHub

  • PR 69511 — Streaming conversational TTS with barge-in. Interrupt the agent while it speaks. Voice interactions become actually conversational. Worth watching.
  • PR 68229 — Desktop /compress cluster salvage. Fixes a compression bug in the desktop app. Clean engineering from ethernet8023.
  • PR 66520 — Migrate CI to GKE self-hosted runners. Infrastructure maturity signal at Nous.

Worth Reading

  • Nous Research in talks for 75M funding at 1.5B valuation (TechCrunch). Robot Ventures leading, USV participating. The valuation validates the agent platform thesis.
  • Hermes Agent 2026: Release Tracker (PetronellaTech). Good reference page bookmark.

New Tutorials

  • Hermes Agent Fundamentals In 29 Minutes Tina Huang. 54K views, 1 day old.
  • 100 hours of Hermes Agent lessons in 19 minutes Alex Finn. 51K views, 10 days old.
  • Hermes Agent Full Course & Setup Guide Tech With Tim. 83K views.
